patterns: minimal-ai: use deterministic logic first, LLMs only when reasoning or generation is required
structured-outputs: always request structured (JSON) outputs from LLMs, validate with Pydantic
chain-of-responsibility: break complex tasks into sequential steps, each independently testable
hybrid-rag-pipeline: ingest → chunk → embed → index (sparse + dense) → parallel retrieve → RRF fuse → cross-encoder rerank → generate → validate
cognitive-architecture: design data flow diagrams before writing AI code
agentic-retrieval: for complex queries (3+ files), use iterative search → read → evaluate → repeat loop instead of single-pass retrieval
modular-primitives: expose discrete list/search/read tools to agent rather than monolithic retrieval blocks
guardrails: prompt injection detection, PII filtering, output validation on every LLM call
fallback-chains: if primary model fails, fall back to secondary, then to deterministic response
structured-citations: every AI-generated answer includes file name, quote, and line number for verifiable traceability
multi-stage-pipeline: decouple ingestion → transformation → agent processing → delivery as independent stages — a failure in one stage must not impede others
specialized-agents: use dedicated agents per task (digest agent for summarization, curator agent for ranking) rather than one monolithic agent doing everything
lightweight-transformation: prefer lightweight tools (Rust-based parsers, native libraries) over heavy ML models for content extraction — avoid OOM on standard cloud instances
principles: The AI layer is a thin wrapper — most of the system is regular software engineering.
Every LLM call is traced (input, output, latency, token cost) via Langfuse.
Never expose raw LLM output to users without validation and sanitization.
Embeddings are generated once at ingestion time, not at query time (except for the query itself).
Hybrid retrieval is mandatory — BM25 for keyword precision + dense vectors for semantic recall, fused with RRF, refined with cross-encoder reranking.
Challenge the Vector Database Myth: for corpora under 1M documents, disk-based numpy arrays + BM25S outperform dedicated vector databases in latency and simplicity.
LLM-as-a-judge for automated evaluation, but always validate against human-annotated datasets.
Provider SDKs are isolated behind a service interface — switching providers should be a config change.
Context windows are finite — chunk documents intelligently, don't stuff everything in.
Start with semantic hybrid RAG — upgrade to agentic RAG only when query complexity demands multi-step reasoning.
Agent tools return errors as messages, never raise exceptions — the LLM interprets and course-corrects autonomously.
Enforce hard limits on agent tool calls and file read sizes to prevent context-window DoS.

rag.pipeline: ingest → chunk → embed → index → hybrid-retrieve → fuse → rerank → generate → validate

chunking.hard_limit: 1000 tokens per chunk — enforced, not advisory. Preserves semantic locality and model compatibility.
principles: Chunk boundaries should respect semantic units (paragraphs, sections) not just token counts.
Every chunk carries enough metadata to trace back to its source document and location.
Chunking strategy is an experimental variable — changes must be validated against NDCG@10.

retrieval.description: Vector-only retrieval is insufficient for production. The system dispatches queries concurrently to sparse (BM25) and dense (vector) retrievers, fuses results with RRF, then reranks with a cross-encoder.

sparse_layer.purpose: Keyword precision — captures exact terms, identifiers, alphanumeric codes, and rare vocabulary that dense models ignore.
process: tokenization: deterministic conversion to token IDs with English stop-word filtering
indexing: vocabulary lookup table with TF-style weighting (term frequency relative to corpus)
sparse_layer.limitation: Cannot handle paraphrasing — serves as high-precision anchor, not standalone retriever.

dense_layer.purpose: Semantic mapping — captures meaning and handles paraphrasing by mapping text to high-dimensional vector space.

optimization.normalization: L2 normalization — rescale vectors to unit Euclidean distance
optimization.similarity: dot-product via numpy matrix multiplication (not cosine_similarity — L2 normalization makes them equivalent, dot product is faster)
optimization.rationale: Pure matrix algebra provides near-instantaneous retrieval across the corpus without dedicated vector database overhead.

persistence.loading: indices loaded directly into memory from disk

fusion.purpose: Scale-agnostic merging of BM25 scores and dense similarity scores (which exist on different numerical scales).
process: 1. concurrent-retrieval: retrieve top-K candidates (K=50) from both BM25 and dense index
2. list-aggregation: collate results into list-of-lists, maintaining original rank per document
3. fused-ranking: apply RRF formula to each document ID in the aggregated lists
4. final-sort: sort by fused scores to produce hybrid ranking
fusion.rationale: The smoothing factor k=60 prevents a single high-ranking result from one retriever from dominating the output, ensuring balanced contribution from both layers.

reranking.purpose: Joint query-document analysis — unlike bi-encoders (which embed query and document separately), cross-encoders analyze them together to capture subtle contextual relevance.
reranking.effect: Contextually mismatched but statistically relevant documents are downgraded; highly relevant documents are promoted to top positions.

additional_techniques: contextual-retrieval: add document context to chunks before embedding for better recall
query-expansion: rephrase user query into multiple variants for broader recall
self-query: extract metadata filters from natural language queries before retrieval

index_persistence.strategy: cached-indexing to minimize latency and recurring API costs
principles: Indices are stored locally and can be version-controlled.
Indices are loaded into memory from disk — no database server required for corpora under 1M documents.
Challenge the Vector Database Myth: for corpora under 1M documents, disk-based numpy arrays outperform dedicated vector databases in latency and operational simplicity.
Transition to managed vector DB (pgvector, Weaviate) only when corpus exceeds 1M documents or multi-tenant isolation is required.

evaluation.description: Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain at Rank 10 — measures retrieval quality based on position of relevant documents in top 10 results.
evaluation.benchmark_reference: BEIR (Benchmarking IR) framework with domain-specific datasets

expected_performance.dense_only: improved semantic recall but misses keyword-dependent identifiers
expected_performance.hybrid_plus_reranking: near 2x improvement over BM25-only (e.g., NDCG@10 ~47 on Financial QA)

metrics: NDCG@10: primary — position-weighted relevance in top 10
retrieval-precision: are the retrieved chunks relevant?
retrieval-recall: did we miss relevant chunks?
answer-faithfulness: is the generated answer grounded in retrieved context?
answer-relevance: does the generated answer address the user's question?

cold_start.description: When proprietary data lacks existing benchmarks, generate synthetic evaluation sets.
cold_start.method: Use high-reasoning LLM to generate BEIR-style (Query, Relationship, Corpus) pairs from document chunks.
cold_start.principle: Never ship a RAG pipeline without an evaluation dataset — synthetic is better than none.

experimental_loop: 1. establish baseline NDCG@10 with current pipeline
2. change one variable (chunking strategy, embedding model, retrieval parameters)
4. keep changes that improve NDCG@10, revert those that don't
5. repeat — every pipeline change is validated scientifically, not by vibes

retrieval_paradigms.description: Two paradigms exist — choose based on query complexity, latency budget, and accuracy requirements.

semantic_rag.correction_capability: none — static output from single retrieval
semantic_rag.best_for: straightforward queries, high-volume low-latency, FAQ-style lookups
semantic_rag.limitation: fails when answer requires synthesizing from multiple sources or when initial retrieval misses the target

agentic_rag.correction_capability: high — autonomous recovery via feedback loop
agentic_rag.best_for: complex queries spanning 3+ files, engineering wikis, codebase navigation, multi-step reasoning
agentic_rag.advantage: mimics human engineer persistence — if first search is irrelevant, agent recognizes failure and pivots

toolkit.description: Modular primitives exposed to the LLM for autonomous file system navigation.
primitives: list: pattern matching (glob) to identify relevant files — use relative paths for token optimization
search (grab): compiled regex for case-insensitive search across document lines — returns file names and line numbers
read: content extraction with path validation (is_relative_to) to prevent directory traversal attacks
toolkit.discovery_pattern: list the big picture → search for the needle → read the context to verify
toolkit.high_performance_search: ripgrep via subprocess — Rust-based, respects .gitignore, ignores hidden directories, gold standard for codebase RAG

context_window_dos_prevention: agent_request_limit: hard cap on tool calls per query to prevent infinite loops
read_max_lines: limit file ingestion size to prevent context exhaustion from large files
timeout: maximum wall-clock time per agent execution
production_safety.error_handling: return human-readable error messages, never raise exceptions in agent tools — treat the LLM as a system administrator that interprets errors and course-corrects

structured_citations.requirement: every answer must include verifiable citations
structured_citations.purpose: enables downstream validation by human auditors and automated verification systems
structured_citations.implementation: Pydantic models enforce citation structure in agent outputs

decision_framework: 1. does the answer require synthesizing from 3+ files? → agentic RAG
2. is target response time milliseconds? → semantic RAG. Can user wait 10-15 seconds? → agentic RAG
3. does deployment support subprocess execution (ripgrep)? If restricted serverless → semantic RAG
4. is there budget for multiple LLM calls per query? If constrained → semantic RAG with hybrid retrieval
5. default: start with semantic hybrid RAG, upgrade to agentic when query complexity demands it

context_architecture.description: Context management is the single biggest lever for AI quality. Minimize token bloat while maximizing relevance.

tiered_loading.description: Three-tier hierarchy to minimize token consumption while preserving navigation ability.
levels: level-0 (abstract): one-line summary per area — agent scans entire system architecture in under 2,000 tokens
level-1 (overview): short description of purpose, workflows, and inter-area relationships
level-2 (full): deep-level data accessed only when agent confirms relevance from level 0-1
tiered_loading.principle: Load the minimum context needed for the agent to decide if it needs more. Never front-load full documents.

context_hub_structure: identity: mission, values, system personality — loaded into every system prompt
inbox: temporary staging for unprocessed ideas and tasks
areas: long-term responsibilities (e.g., clients, products, operations)
knowledge: permanent reference library — SOPs, documentation, domain guides
archive: stale data — agents are explicitly instructed to ignore this to prevent context clutter
context_architecture.storage: Git-versioned Markdown files — version-controlled, diffable, editable locally and synced to cloud
principles: Context is King — the quality of AI output is determined by the quality of context, not the model.
Separate structured data (PostgreSQL) from unstructured context (Markdown/Git).
Agent skills and instructions are defined in version-controlled Markdown files — installable, removable, auditable.
Every agent interaction starts with identity context (who am I, what are my values) before task context.

context_engineering.description: The strategic orchestration of the entire information environment at inference time. Not just what you prompt — everything that lands in the context window.

token_economy.principle: Treat the context window as a finite resource — like working memory. Never treat it as infinite storage.
degradation_modes: needle-in-haystack: reasoning reliability drops as context expands — the model's ability to pinpoint specific facts becomes non-deterministic in large windows
instruction-atrophy: bloated windows cause the model to neglect core system prompt rules, prioritizing recent tokens over foundational instructions
rule-neglect: by Turn 10+, most agents begin to forget instructions or reason over outdated data
smallest_set_mandate: prune: programmatically remove irrelevant, redundant, or outdated turns from conversation history
summarize: compress early segments (e.g., replace first 20 messages with a single state summary) to free tokens for current reasoning
rank: broad retrieval (50 chunks) → reranker → inject only top 5-10 high-signal chunks into the prompt

system_prompt_calibration.description: Avoid the Calibration Trap — don't fix every user complaint by hardcoding edge cases into the prompt.

specificity_spectrum.too_vague: insufficient constraints → off-brand behavior and hallucinations
specificity_spectrum.too_specific: every edge case hardcoded as if-else logic → unscalable context bloat, model ignores core rules
specificity_spectrum.sweet_spot: structured behavioral constraints with positive few-shot examples

positive_over_negative.principle: LLMs are statistically poor at processing negative constraints ('Don't do X'). Provide a picture of success instead.
positive_over_negative.pattern: Replace lists of prohibitions with multi-turn positive examples that anchor the model's behavior
positive_over_negative.example: Instead of 'Do not use technical jargon', provide a sample conversation showing the desired plain-language style

modular_architecture.description: Decompose complex tasks into modular sub-problems to reduce cognitive load on the model.

workflow_vs_agent.strict_workflows: deterministic routing and prompt chaining — for customer-facing support or backend automations where humans are not in the loop. Highly reliable.
workflow_vs_agent.autonomous_agents: tools in a loop for open-ended problems — powerful for human-in-the-loop scenarios. WARNING: avoid fully autonomous agents for backend tasks without strict deterministic guards.

tool_engineering: concise-manifests: keep tool descriptions short — focused on 'what' and 'when' to call
eliminate-redundancy: tools must have non-overlapping purposes — overlapping tools create confusion during tool-selection inference
sub-agent-delegation: rather than exposing 10 tools to one agent, use a router to offload tasks to specialized sub-agents with limited, high-signal toolsets
mcp-first: expose Python functions as MCP tools for modular, composable, conversationally accessible capabilities
tool-chaining: design MCP servers as composable units — each does one thing well, chain them for complex workflows via a single prompt

dynamic_context_management.description: Static context fails in long-running interactions. Context must be actively managed at runtime.

state_machine_injection.pattern: Track user journey state in database, not in LLM memory. Based on current state, pull only the relevant system prompt or context block at runtime.
state_machine_injection.benefit: Prevents the model from being distracted by instructions for future or past states.
state_machine_injection.implementation: Database-backed state with runtime context swapping — each state maps to a specific prompt fragment

memory_management: conversation-pruning: replace old messages with state summaries as conversation grows
sliding-window: keep only the last N turns plus a compressed summary of everything before
selective-injection: only inject tool outputs and retrieved data relevant to the current reasoning step

failure_analysis_hierarchy.description: When an agent fails, follow this checklist BEFORE attempting to fix the prompt.
steps: 1. analyze-trace: inspect the full message history to see what the model actually 'saw' at the moment of failure
2. reasoning-over-context: was the failure caused by too much noise, too little signal, or outdated information?
3. identify-source: is the issue in RAG retrieval, a tool description, the conversation history, or the system prompt?
4. inject-positive-examples: only after trace analysis should you refine the prompt, using positive few-shot examples
failure_analysis_hierarchy.principle: Context is often hidden during development but explodes in production as interactions deepen. Trace before you prompt-fix.

multi_stage_pipelines.description: Production AI applications are staged pipelines, not monolithic scripts. Each stage is independently deployable, testable, and recoverable.
stages: 1. ingestion: consolidate heterogeneous data sources (APIs, RSS, webhooks, scrapers) into a common schema — prioritize low-latency strategies (XML feeds over browser scraping)
2. transformation: convert raw data to LLM-ready format (HTML → Markdown, PDF → text) — use lightweight tools to avoid OOM on standard instances
3. agent-processing: specialized agents operate on transformed data — one agent per task (summarizer, ranker, curator, classifier)
4. persistence: store all outputs with state-management flags (processed_at, delivered_at) — enables idempotent reruns and prevents redundant processing
5. delivery: automated distribution (email/SMTP, webhooks, API responses) — close the loop without requiring user to check a dashboard
design_principles: decoupled-stages: an ingestion failure must not impede processing of previously cached data
common-schema: all sources normalized into a unified data model before agent processing
specialized-agents: Digest Agent (summarize), Curator Agent (rank against user profile) — never one monolithic agent doing everything
content-transformation-decisions: test on target cloud instance before committing to heavy tools — prefer Rust-based parsers over Python OCR libraries when memory is constrained
deterministic-over-generative: use deterministic logic for routing, deduplication, state management — reserve LLM calls for tasks that genuinely require reasoning

llm_evaluation.description: Systematic evaluation framework for non-deterministic AI outputs. Moves from accidental success to controlled engineering.
virtuous_cycle: 1. evaluation-quality: systematic measurements via assertions, HITL reviews, and alignment metrics
2. debugging-traceability: Langfuse trace logging to diagnose whether failure originated in retrieval, logic, or prompting
3. behavior-change: iterative prompt and architecture refinement — failures converted into permanent improvements

reference_based.description: Used when a known correct answer (golden answer) exists — easily automated as Level 1 unit tests.
types: exact-match: string or JSON matching against reference
semantic-similarity: vector distance between output and reference answer
code-correctness: generated queries/code are syntactically valid and produce expected results

reference_free.description: Used for subjective quality where multiple valid answers exist — requires Level 2 judgment.
types: tone-appropriateness: empathetic resonance or professional alignment
safety: toxic or harmful content detection
constraint-compliance: adherence to formatting, length, and structural limits

metric_selection.binary_first_mandate: Use Pass/Fail before scalar scores. Scalar scores (e.g., 4.2/5 helpfulness) introduce noise and make Human-Model Agreement impossible to calculate. Binary + qualitative reasoning is the only actionable engineering insight.

llm_as_judge.description: Use a high-performing model to automate critique of application outputs. The judge model must be more capable than the generator.
llm_as_judge.recommended_models: GPT-4, Claude Sonnet 4.6+ — always use the most powerful available model for judging
validation_workflow: 1. prediction-collection: gather model outputs from real or synthetic data
2. critique-generation: use Judge LLM to evaluate those outputs
3. human-evaluation: domain expert evaluates the same data independently
4. judgment-comparison: identify discrepancies between Model Judge and Human
5. evaluator-iteration: refine Judge instructions based on gaps
6. repeat: iterate until Model and Human consistently align

judge_prompt_requirements.dimensions: specifically define Accuracy, Helpfulness, and Tone as separate evaluation axes
judge_prompt_requirements.chain_of_thought: mandate CoT reasoning before score — 'Analyze step-by-step against reference criteria. Provide reasoning first, then issue binary 1 (Pass) or 0 (Fail).'
judge_prompt_requirements.structured_output: judge must return JSON/Pydantic-validated output — never free-text scores

hitl_alignment_protocol.description: Domain experts provide the gold standard target. Tool-first thinking fails because it ignores business context that defines 'good'.

agreement_matrix.purpose: Track alignment between Judge model and Human expert. Human is always the ultimate authority.
agreement_matrix.rule: If alignment score is 0 (mismatch), the human is always right — the Judge prompt must be fixed.

meta_prompting.description: Feed discrepancies (cases where Human and Judge disagreed) into a high-level model.
meta_prompting.purpose: Identify patterns in human critique and automatically suggest refinements to the Judge's system prompt.
meta_prompting.result: Sophisticated feedback loop that mathematically aligns the automated evaluator with expert intuition.

failure_mode_collection.description: AI systems suffer from Data Drift — user behavior and model performance shift over time. The evaluation system must evolve.
failure_mode_collection.raw_events: Capture exact JSON data flowing through the system. When a production failure is identified via Langfuse or user feedback, isolate it as a Level 1 unit test.
failure_mode_collection.principle: Fix locally, redeploy, the same failure mode never recurs. Every production failure becomes a permanent regression test.
failure_mode_collection.manual_inspection: Manual trace inspection has the highest value-to-prestige ratio in AI engineering. Even at companies like Zapier, Principal Engineers spend up to 3 hours daily looking at traces. Automation does not replace the need to look at the data.

key_principles: Start simple: master binary Pass/Fail before complex scoring.
Build domain-specific: avoid generic off-the-shelf metrics — evaluations must reflect specific business logic.
Remove friction: viewing, labeling, and analyzing data must be effortless for engineers and experts.
Iterate systematically: use feedback loops to ensure improvements compound, not regress.

key_libraries: langchain: LLM orchestration, chains, document loaders, text splitters
langgraph: stateful agent workflows with cycles and branching
langfuse: LLM observability, tracing, prompt management
tiktoken: token counting for context window management
bm25s: sparse retrieval with BM25 scoring (Lucene method)
numpy: high-performance vector operations, index persistence (.npy)
pydantic-ai: tool-call orchestration with strict data validation for agent outputs
ripgrep: Rust-based high-performance search for agentic RAG (subprocess, respects .gitignore)
